pageMetadata.title: Hook | Unified AI Gateway
pageMetadata.description: Unify OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, and CLI traffic behind one entry point, with routing, token governance, cost quotas, and health monitoring in one control plane.

hero.description: Unify Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, and CLI tool traffic behind one entry point, with model routing, user tokens, cost quotas, and health monitoring in a single control plane.

code.prompt: Call a unified AI gateway through Hook

models.title: Connect 300+ models
models.description: Bring OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, DeepSeek, Qwen, and other leading or domestic model providers behind one entry point for chat, embeddings, image, and audio workloads.

tokens.title: Token and access control
tokens.description: Issue API tokens to users and teams, then control model access by group and role.

routing.title: Smart load balancing
routing.description: Route across multiple provider endpoints with priority, weight, and health status, and switch automatically during failures.

deployment.title: Self-hosted deployment
deployment.description: Start with Docker Compose, or build from source when you want full control. Keep your data and credentials in your own environment.

monitoring.title: Health monitoring and audit
monitoring.description: Track success rate, latency, and provider state in real time, with logs and request records ready when you need them.

quota.title: Cost and quota control
quota.description: Record usage and cost by user, group, and model, then block traffic explicitly when budgets run out.

cursor.models: Point Cursor to the Hook gateway

liveDemo.title: See Hook in action
liveDemo.subtitle: Simulate multi-provider access, highly available load balancing, and automatic failover in seconds

controls.failoverDescription: Force the primary channel to fail and rehearse automatic failover through the gateway

liveDemo.emptyState: Waiting for a request... Click “Send gateway request” on the left to inspect the forwarding trace

gateway.label: What is an AI gateway?
gateway.text: What is an AI gateway, and what are its core advantages?

joke.label: Tell a programmer joke
joke.text: Tell me a joke about programmers and bugs.

rust.label: Write Rust Hello World
rust.text: Write the most basic Hello World example in Rust.

gpt4o.gateway: An AI gateway is a unified proxy layer between clients and upstream AI services. It handles authentication, model routing, smart load balancing, rate control, auditing, and automatic failover so teams reduce key exposure and switch traffic within seconds during outages.
gpt4o.joke: Why do programmers prefer dark mode? Because light attracts bugs.

claude.gateway: An AI gateway is a one-stop solution for multi-model integration. Instead of exposing every provider difference to application code, it hides protocol and pricing differences behind a standard interface and distributes traffic by priority or weight.
claude.joke: There are 10 kinds of people in the world: those who understand binary, and those who do not.

gemini.gateway: An AI gateway is a high-performance middleware layer that shields applications from differences in streaming, error handling, and billing across model providers. It pulls cost control, security, and availability into one request path.
gemini.joke: A programmer went shopping. Their partner said, “Buy a loaf of bread, and if they have watermelons, buy one.” The programmer came back with one loaf of bread because the store did have watermelons.

deepseek.gateway:
<think>
Thought process:
1. The user is asking for the definition and value of an AI gateway.
2. Summarize the key problems: protocol fragmentation, credential sprawl, and missing failover.
3. Answer in a structured, enterprise-oriented way.
</think>
An AI gateway is key infrastructure for enterprise AI systems. It unifies API formats across major providers and provides unified routing, smart load balancing, and failover so traffic can move safely across multiple upstream channels.
deepseek.joke:
<think>
Thought process:
1. Pick a classic programmer joke.
2. Use a base-conversion joke for instant recognition.
</think>
Why do programmers always mix up Halloween and Christmas? Because Oct 31 equals Dec 25.

</think>


custom.deepseek:
<think>
Reasoning:
The user entered a custom prompt: "{{prompt}}". The gateway maps the request and forwards it to the configured {{model}} upstream.
</think>
[Hook Gateway] This is a simulated answer for the custom prompt. In production, Hook would route the request to your configured {{model}} provider and stream back the real result.
custom.default:
[Hook Gateway] Received custom input: "{{prompt}}".
This is a preview environment. In production, the request would be routed safely through Hook to the configured {{model}} upstream and billed against your token and quota plan.

logs.quotaChecked: Checked quota and wallet balance... healthy. Remaining balance: $84.21
logs.primarySelected: Smart load balancing: selected primary channel A [Official OpenAI - weight 50%]
logs.primaryFailed: Channel A failed: [HTTP 503] Service Unavailable (upstream rate limit or outage)
logs.failoverTriggered: Gateway failover triggered: the primary request failed, switching to automatic retry strategy.
logs.secondarySelected: Smart load balancing: selected backup channel B [Azure OpenAI backup - priority 2]
logs.secondarySuccess: Channel B returned 200 OK and established a Server-Sent Events stream.
logs.primarySuccess: Channel A returned 200 OK and established a Server-Sent Events stream.

quickStart.title: Start deployment in seconds
quickStart.hint: Self-hosted deployment keeps your data and credentials in your own environment.

providers.title: Supported provider channels and ecosystem integrations
providers.subtitle: Built-in cross-provider protocol rewriting wraps heterogeneous APIs into one standard format for mainstream clients and developer frameworks

midjourney.description: Imagine, Upscale, and Variation image workflows

groq.description: LLaMA 3.1 and Mixtral for low-latency inference

cta.title: Take control of your AI traffic
cta.description: Deploy Hook in one step and run model routing, load balancing, token throttling, and observability in a secure self-hosted environment. Open source, free, and private by default.
